_note: RAG Framework descriptor wording. Edit in Settings > Framework Editor. Changes apply everywhere immediately.

levels.label: Confident
levels.summary: Progressing independently. Peer mentoring potential. Case study ready.

levels.label: On Track
levels.summary: Engaged and progressing. Specific gaps identified. Fortnightly coaching.

levels.label: Developing
levels.summary: Wants to improve, lacks clarity or structure. Weekly coaching + training.

levels.label: Challenged
levels.summary: Significant barriers. Intensive support. HoA partnership essential.

levels.label: Urgent
levels.summary: Fundamental blockage. VP Quality escalation needed.

dimensions.name: Staff Digital Capability & Confidence
dimensions.dataSource: Jisc Discovery Tool results, 1:1 conversations, Learning Walk observations
dimensions.coachingQuestion: On the Pyramid, where are the teaching staff in this area?

descriptors.1: Staff use digital tools independently and confidently across all areas of practice. Peer mentoring happens organically. Staff advocate for new tools and share practice. Digital is embedded, not bolted on.
descriptors.2: Staff are engaged and progressing. Most use M365 confidently. Specific tool gaps identified (e.g. CRM, specialist software). Some staff need targeted training. Willingness to develop is high.
descriptors.3: Staff use basic tools but confidence is inconsistent. Some resistance or uncertainty. Digital use is surface-level — PowerPoints and files, not deeper pedagogical integration. Training required.
descriptors.4: Staff confidence is low across the board. Significant knowledge gaps in M365 basics. Some staff actively resistant. Infrastructure or device issues compound the challenge. Intensive 1:1 support needed.
descriptors.5: Staff have minimal or no digital practice. No baseline established. Leadership has not prioritised digital CPD. Structural barriers prevent progress. Escalation to VP Quality required.

dimensions.name: HoA Leadership Understanding & Planning
dimensions.alignedTo: Quality cycle, QIP, CPD levers in Quality Handbook
dimensions.dataSource: HoA conversations, QIP review, budget allocation, CPD calendar
dimensions.coachingQuestion: Does the HoA own digital improvement or is it something the DPC does?

descriptors.1: HoA owns digital strategy. Can articulate specific gaps, has a written plan, tracks progress. Digital is embedded in QIP. CPD budget allocated. Regular check-ins with DL without prompting.
descriptors.2: HoA is engaged and understands the direction. Aware of key gaps. Plan in development. Needs fortnightly coaching to maintain momentum and translate awareness into action.
descriptors.3: HoA sees digital as important but hasn't translated this into a plan. Relies on DPC to drive agenda. Some uncertainty about where to start. Weekly coaching and structured planning needed.
descriptors.4: HoA has limited awareness of digital gaps. Digital not embedded in QIP. No CPD plan. May see digital as 'IT's job'. Needs intensive support and a clear, simple entry point.
descriptors.5: HoA has no digital strategy and no ownership. Digital improvement not prioritised. Structural or relational barriers present. VP Quality conversation needed to establish accountability.

dimensions.name: Digital Skills Assessment & Tracking
dimensions.alignedTo: Century Tech EDS diagnostics, specialist skills mapping, Markbook integration
dimensions.dataSource: Century dashboard, HoA specialist skills tracking table, course tracking documentation
dimensions.coachingQuestion: Can you pull a report showing each learner's digital skills starting point and progress?

descriptors.1: Century Initial Assessment embedded at entry for all learners. Specialist digital skills mapped per course. Progress tracked in Markbook. HoA can produce evidence of digital skills progress on request.
descriptors.2: Century IA used but not consistently. Specialist skills mapping started or in progress. Some progress tracked. Gaps in consistency across staff or courses.
descriptors.3: Awareness of Century and tracking systems. Some use but not embedded. Specialist skills not yet mapped. Tracking is informal or ad hoc. Needs structured approach.
descriptors.4: Little or no use of Century for digital skills baseline. No specialist skills tracking. Staff unsure what to assess or how. Significant gap between current practice and expectation.
descriptors.5: No digital skills assessment or tracking in place. HoA cannot evidence learner digital progress. Not on the radar. Foundational work needed before tracking is possible.

dimensions.name: Digital Curriculum Integration
dimensions.alignedTo: UDL principles, QFT, Digital Learning Pyramid Level 2 (Inclusion)
dimensions.dataSource: Assignment review, Learning Walk observations, feedback on accessible design
dimensions.coachingQuestion: When you look at a unit of work, is digital essential or optional?

descriptors.1: Digital is embedded in curriculum design. Assignments require digital skills as well as subject skills. UDL principles applied. Resources are accessible by design. AT is offered proactively.
descriptors.2: Digital is used purposefully in most sessions. Some assignment design reflects digital skills. Accessibility is considered but not yet systematic. Good practice visible in pockets.
descriptors.3: Digital is used but often as a delivery tool (slides, files) rather than a learning tool. Assignment design does not yet require or track digital skills. Accessibility is reactive.
descriptors.4: Digital is peripheral. Sessions could happen identically without technology. Assignments don't reference digital skills. Staff unsure how to integrate meaningfully. Training and curriculum redesign needed.
descriptors.5: No evidence of digital curriculum integration. Technology use is incidental or absent. Curriculum design does not account for digital skills development. Foundational redesign required.

dimensions.name: Digital Health — Accessibility & Inclusion
dimensions.alignedTo: Learning Without Barriers (thread through all Pyramid levels), WCAG 2.2 AA
dimensions.dataSource: Learning Walks, Digital Health Check observations, learner feedback on accessibility
dimensions.coachingQuestion: Can you see inclusion in action in a learning session?

descriptors.1: WCAG 2.2 AA practice is the norm. Staff proactively offer AT. Immersive Reader, Live Captions, alt text are standard. Learners know what support is available. Inclusive design is non-negotiable.
descriptors.2: Staff are aware of accessibility tools and use them with some consistency. Accessibility Checker used on most resources. AT offered when asked. Some gaps in proactive practice.
descriptors.3: Some accessibility awareness but practice is inconsistent. Resources not systematically checked. AT not proactively offered. Staff know the tools exist but haven't embedded them.
descriptors.4: Significant accessibility gaps. Resources not accessible by design. AT not offered or not known about by staff. Learners may be experiencing barriers that aren't visible to staff. Urgent Teach-Meet needed.
descriptors.5: No accessible practice visible. Staff unaware of AT options. Resources inaccessible. Learners experiencing digital exclusion. Liz Burkley / Lois Knight partnership and VP escalation required.

dimensions.name: Digital Lead Engagement & Health Checks
dimensions.alignedTo: Digital Leads Training Plan 2025–27, Health Check framework
dimensions.dataSource: DL engagement conversation, Health Check schedule, peer coaching observations
dimensions.coachingQuestion: Is the DL your eyes and ears in the area, or waiting for guidance?

descriptors.1: DL is fully identified, trained, and active. Running Health Checks independently. Mentoring peers. Providing weekly updates. Ready to mentor DLs from other areas.
descriptors.2: DL identified and engaged. Attending check-ins. Health Checks starting. Some peer coaching happening. Needs fortnightly support to maintain momentum.
descriptors.3: DL identified but not yet active in Health Checks or peer support. Willing but unclear on role or lacking confidence. Needs structured training and close weekly support.
descriptors.4: DL nominally identified but not functioning in the role. No Health Checks. No peer engagement. May need role clarification or a different person. HoA needs to reinforce the role.
descriptors.5: No DL identified. No engagement. Area has no internal digital champion. Fundamental blocker. HoA conversation essential. Cannot progress without this in place.
